verification_gate: One bounded source slice completes source-to-database-to-projection-to-reproducible-build-to-running-UDS round trip with byte, identity, behavior, provenance, rollback, and rebuild proofs.

bounded_source_slice_defined.membership: checksummed include manifest (SHA-256 + BLAKE3), not a directory glob
exclusions: media/communications/domain records not needed to answer one request

stage_source_to_database.mechanism: Nushell AST parse -> nu_plugin redb buffer (MessagePack, BLAKE3 dedup) -> envctl embed (all-MiniLM-L6-v2) + commit raw_code/semantic_embedding/module_path/causal edges to bounded PostgreSQL branch
stage_source_to_database.canonical_owner: PostgreSQL + RuVector (durable + semantic); redb transient forward buffer

stage_database_to_projection.mechanism: envctl four-step: query production branch -> module_path map -> concatenate raw_code -> dump clean disposable directory
stage_database_to_projection.canonical_owner: PostgreSQL source of truth; envctl read-only projection; filesystem disposable

stage_projection_to_reproducible_build.mechanism: Nix deterministic compile against x86_64-unknown-linux-musl (strict static, no /nix/store RPATH), gitkb/meta scoped to pure Rust, packed install-in-place folder
stage_projection_to_reproducible_build.canonical_owner: binary/closure is a derived, regenerable projection; PostgreSQL remains authority

stage_build_to_running_uds.mechanism: launch portable folder (Yazelix inits Zellij+Nushell) -> bind Unix Domain Socket -> answer one lifeos.status request from canonical PostgreSQL identities
stage_build_to_running_uds.canonical_owner: LifeOS control surface maps to canonical PG identities (POSTGRES-007); no HTTP/REST; no hidden filesystem control-write

byte_proof.criteria: SHA-256(projected member) == SHA-256(source member) for all 3 members; two independent projection runs byte-identical (POSTGRES-008); byte_len and mode preserved

identity_proof.criteria: bijection source-block <-> PostgreSQL row <-> projected byte-range via (module_path, SHA-256); each member in exactly one STORE-001 class; redb never a durable second writer

behavior_proof.criteria: run_receipt shows lifeos.status answered with status==ok; every response field resolves to a canonical PostgreSQL identity/receipt; exit_status==0

provenance_proof.criteria: each slice row carries source commit+PR (adopted_capabilities.source.csv), a causal dependency edge, and a SHAKE256 witness; Causal Graph Verification rejects rootless blocks; zero rootless blocks in slice

rollback_proof.criteria: drop bounded PostgreSQL namespace/branch + remove projected directory + remove build folder by receipt id; original source checksums unchanged; installed profile untouched; rollback_receipt records zero destructive action on source/profile

rebuild_proof.criteria: two independent S2->S3 runs from the same source_version_id produce identical build_binary_sha256; projection byte-identical; re-embedding reproducible under pinned model + BLAKE3 key
rebuild_proof.evidence_source: projection_receipt + build_receipt across two runs; embedding_model_pin

store_001_alignment.single_durable_writer: PostgreSQL owns every durable macro-state class in the slice exactly once; redb buffer/dedup only
store_001_alignment.no_sidecar: RuVector native PostgreSQL extension; semantic column in PostgreSQL
store_001_alignment.verified_cutover: exercises the intended path against a disposable bounded namespace; no silent redb-durable reclassification
store_001_alignment.provenance_preserved: SHA-256 identity + commit/PR lineage + SHAKE256 witness kept for every block

proof_summary: POSTGRES-009 defines the smallest end-to-end round-trip proof for a portable projected LifeOS build and shows every gate clause is covered by a concrete, blueprint-grounded contract. The bounded slice postgres009-uds-probe is one crate with three content-addressed members (Cargo.toml, src/main.rs, src/uds.rs) and exactly one lifeos.status UDS request; membership is a checksummed include manifest with secrets/** and AgentDB .rvf explicitly excluded. Four round-trip stages each carry one owner and one receipt: S1 source-to-database runs the blueprint's three pillars (Nushell AST -> nu_plugin redb buffer with MessagePack + BLAKE3 dedup -> envctl embed via all-MiniLM-L6-v2 -> PostgreSQL commit of raw_code/semantic_embedding/module_path/causal edges); S2 database-to-projection runs envctl's four-step materialization into a disposable byte-deterministic directory; S3 projection-to-reproducible-build compiles deterministically with Nix against x86_64-unknown-linux-musl, gitkb/meta-scoped to pure Rust, into an install-in-place folder; S4 build-to-running-UDS launches the folder (Yazelix inits Zellij+Nushell), binds a Unix Domain Socket, and answers the one request from canonical PostgreSQL identities with no HTTP/REST and no hidden filesystem control-write. All six required proofs receive concrete acceptance criteria: byte (projected==source SHA-256 and cross-run byte-identical projection), identity (bijection source-block <-> PostgreSQL row <-> projected byte-range via module_path+SHA-256, each of the six STORE-001 state classes assigned exactly once), behavior (UDS request answered, fields identity-mapped, clean exit), provenance (commit/PR lineage from adopted_capabilities.source.csv + SHAKE256 witness + causal edge, zero rootless blocks), rollback (drop bounded namespace + remove projection + remove build folder by receipt id, source and installed profile unchanged), and rebuild (byte-identical binary and reproducible re-embedding from the same PostgreSQL version). STORE-001 alignment holds throughout: PostgreSQL+RuVector is the single durable/semantic owner, redb is a forward-only transient buffer, envctl is the bridge and projection engine, RuVector is a No-Sidecar native extension, and every block keeps its SHA-256 identity and lineage; the one open dependency (live RuVector-in-PostgreSQL extension activation not yet locally proven) is handled per STORE-001 by producing the slice's embeddings via the verified Bun ruvector N-API path while PostgreSQL keeps column ownership. A validator confirmed both design artifacts are valid JSON, all four round-trip stages and all six proofs are present, the slice has three members and one UDS request, all six STORE-001 state classes are covered, and the task family carries seven work_items with id/surface/owner/decision. No live database mutation, projection, build, or run was performed; this is a definition gate and product_execution_performed is false.

live_commands.purpose: Validate POSTGRES-009 definition gate: both artifacts are valid JSON, all 4 round-trip stages and all 6 proofs present, slice has 3 members + 1 UDS request, all 6 STORE-001 state classes covered, task family work_items carry id/surface/owner/decision
output: all_6_proofs=True proofs=['byte proof','identity proof','behavior proof','provenance proof','rollback proof','rebuild proof']
